risks: Online endpoint has publicNetworkAccess Enabled and authMode Key — best practice is to disable public access and use managed identity with a workspace managed VNet
Single-region deployment (eastus only) with no failover — Cosmos DB locations array contains only one region, offering no geo-redundancy
Storage account uses Standard_LRS which provides no zone redundancy for ML workspace artifacts
No managed identity configured for cross-service authentication between ML workspace, endpoint, and Cosmos DB
Application Insights is missing a Log Analytics workspace dependency (required for workspace-based Application Insights)
No VNet, private endpoint, or network isolation configured for any resource
No explicit integration path between the ML online endpoint and Cosmos DB for writing usage activity logs
correctionsRecommended: Set publicNetworkAccess to Disabled on the online endpoint and enable workspace managed VNet for network isolation
Change authMode from Key to managed identity (AADToken) on the online endpoint
Add a second Cosmos DB region (e.g., westus2) with failoverPriority 1 for geo-redundancy
Upgrade storage account SKU from Standard_LRS to Standard_ZRS for zone-redundant artifact storage
Add a Microsoft.OperationalInsights/workspaces resource and reference it from the Application Insights component
Configure a system-assigned or user-assigned managed identity on the ML workspace with appropriate RBAC roles for Key Vault, Storage, ACR, and Cosmos DB
Disable key-based authentication on the Cosmos DB account and Storage account in favor of RBAC-based access
Consider adding diagnostic settings and a resource tagging strategy for operational excellence
